$comment: Honest disclosure of all network/AI usage in Lantern, for the QVAC 'Unleash Edge AI' judging. Short answer: NO cloud AI. Every inference runs on-device via @qvac/sdk.

aiInference.notes: All language, vision, OCR, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, translation, and embedding inference runs locally through @qvac/sdk. There are no calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or any hosted model API.

modelDownload.what: First-run download of QVAC model weights
modelDownload.when: Once per model; cached under ~/.qvac/models and reused fully offline thereafter

p2pDelegation.what: Optional offload of the heavy vision model to a peer YOU control (the 'Lantern Hub')
p2pDelegation.default: disabled (p2p.delegateVision=false). When enabled, inference goes only to your own hub, never a third-party server, and always falls back to local.
p2pDelegation.containsUserData: Only the specific image being described, sent encrypted to your own device; never to a third party.

network.outboundUserContent: none by default; the audit log never records photos, audio, or recognized text (content-free fingerprints only).

summary: Lantern is edge-AI by construction: the camera never leaves the user's hand, and no AI runs in the cloud.
